---
title: ""
toc: false
page-layout: full
---

::: {.hero}
[COMPANION // FAccT '26]{.eyebrow}

# AI for Emergency Management — Resources for Researchers {.hero-title}

[Use cases, challenges, and opportunities from *Investigating the Role of AI in Emergency Management* — turned into a project picker and onboarding kit for academic researchers who want to support emergency management problems.]{.lede}

::: {.readout}
[32]{.rnum} interviewed emergency managers&nbsp;·&nbsp; [7]{.rnum} use-case categories&nbsp;·&nbsp; [136]{.rnum} specific use cases
:::

[Open the catalog →](catalog.qmd){.btn-primary} [Read the paper](https://doi.org/10.1145/3805689.3812279){.btn-ghost}
:::

## Start here

::: {.cards}

<div class="card-link">
<a class="card-link-overlay" href="catalog.qmd" aria-label="Use-Case Catalog"></a>
<span class="cn">01 / CATALOG</span>
<div class="ct">Use-Case Catalog</div>
<div class="cd">All 136 use cases, filterable by category, EM phase, feasibility, and current AI use — each with an "open problems" column.</div>
</div>

<div class="card-link">
<a class="card-link-overlay" href="literacy.qmd" aria-label="Domain Literacy"></a>
<span class="cn">02 / DOCTRINE</span>
<div class="ct">Domain Literacy</div>
<div class="cd">NIMS/ICS, the Whole Community doctrine, EMAP standards, a sample ICS-201/IAP, and the staffing + funding evidence base.</div>
</div>

<div class="card-link">
<a class="card-link-overlay" href="meet-an-em.qmd" aria-label="Meet an EM"></a>
<span class="cn">03 / PEOPLE</span>
<div class="ct">Meet an EM</div>
<div class="cd">How to engage practitioners ethically: where to find them, what a ride-along or tabletop involves, and the norms that matter.</div>
</div>

<div class="card-link">
<a class="card-link-overlay" href="anti-patterns.qmd" aria-label="Anti-Patterns"></a>
<span class="cn">04 / PITFALLS</span>
<div class="ct">Anti-Patterns</div>
<div class="cd">The rabbit holes — activation prediction, autonomous life-safety decisions, misinformation-detection overpromises — and why.</div>
</div>

<div class="card-link">
<a class="card-link-overlay" href="instruments.qmd" aria-label="Reusable Instruments"></a>
<span class="cn">05 / METHOD</span>
<div class="ct">Reusable Instruments</div>
<div class="cd">The interview protocol and codebook, offered for replication — including the international extension the paper invites.</div>
</div>

<div class="card-link">
<a class="card-link-overlay" href="datasets.qmd" aria-label="Datasets Starter Pack"></a>
<span class="cn">06 / DATA</span>
<div class="ct">Datasets Starter Pack</div>
<div class="cd">Coming soon.</div>
</div>

:::

## The four phases

Emergency management runs on a cyclical four-phase model. Throughout this companion, phase chips mark which part of the cycle a use case touches.

::: {.phase-legend}
[Mitigation]{.chip .chip-mitigate} [Preparedness]{.chip .chip-prepare} [Response]{.chip .chip-respond} [Recovery]{.chip .chip-recover}
:::

::: {.callout-tip}
## How to cite
Foubert, S., Park, M., Pardeshi, K. S., Manzini, T., McDaniel, R., Merrick, D., Murphy, R., Singh, A., & Heidari, H. (2026). *Investigating the Role of AI in Emergency Management: Use Cases, Challenges, and Opportunities.* FAccT '26. <https://doi.org/10.1145/3805689.3812279>
:::
